body: 17 of Irans 26 players have not played competitive football since early February since the Persian gulf league was canceled bec of the war. Friendlies canceled leading up to the World Cup. Logistical restrictions on days leading up to matches. Oldest squad in the tournament. Kept several top quality players off the score sheet today. Wasn’t the best offensive performance by team Melli, but defensively they were stellar. They deserve so much credit.
Belgium has fallen hard, doesn’t seem like that long ago they had the golden generation.

I don't know what everyone's complaining about, I found this match extremely enjoyable.

Iran's defending was insane, and it was curious to see how they dealt with being one man up. Their entire gameplan was to defend and counter, and suddenly they had more spaces?

They clearly didn't trust their luck, and played it (relatively) safe, which I think is totally understandable - even with a man down, Belgium had the players to finish off Iran.

Glad they didn't!

The draws just show the group is quite evenly matched and the last MD will have plenty of entertainment with Belgium possibly getting grouped.

The last solid iran shot was so frustrating to watch as the closest Iranian just stood there butching about not getting the ball when if he had crashed the goal on the shot he would have had an excellent chance at a rebound chance goal, while an Iranian player 10 meters further almost got to the ball. So idiotic that this mindset permeants the game at this level. Dude gets three matches at a world cup in his life, most likely, and he stands around complaining about not getting the ball instead of working harder than the opponent. You see this every match too whether it is people moaning about not getting the ball or rolling around acting like they just got their leg snapped in half(so ridiculous when you compare it to people who actually get hurt, as flailing about when you have a leg broken generally is a bad idea lol) when they could get up and work to make a difference and create space for their team

With this result, Shia-majority Middle Eastern countries have tied Sunni-majority Middle Eastern countries, with two points each:

Sunni:

Qatar, 1
Turkey, 0
Saudi Arabia, 1
Jordan, 0

Shia:

Iran, 2
Iraq, 0

Jordan plays Algeria and Iraq plays France, meaning Sunnis have a chance to regain their advantage.
